title: Same-Race Undercut/Overcut Gain Features
summary: Official F1 strategy analysis emphasizes that pit timing depends on the balance between worn-tyre degradation and fresh-tyre warm-up penalty, and that this balance changes by circuit, car, and race phase.
rationale: Official F1 strategy analysis emphasizes that pit timing depends on the balance between worn-tyre degradation and fresh-tyre warm-up penalty, and that this balance changes by circuit, car, and race phase. The current features model wear, but not race-specific fresh-tyre warm-up asymmetry.
implementation_hint: Using only laps earlier than the current lap, estimate same-race fresh-tyre out-lap penalty and old-tyre in-lap decay from other drivers who have already pitted in the same Year-Race. Derive features like expected_undercut_gain, expected_overcut_gain, fresh_tyre_warmup_penalty, and clean_air_rejoin_proxy.
expected_effect: Improves track-specific tactical calls, particularly where overcut is viable because fresh tyres warm slowly or degradation is low.
risk: Sparse early-race evidence and noisy estimates on races with few observed stops. Needs backoff to global compound-level estimates.
